title: श्री मातङ्गी कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री मातङ्गी कवचम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
श्यामां विद्यां त्रिनयनां चन्द्रखण्डविभूषिताम्।
वीणावादनतत्परां सदा मातङ्गीं नमामि च॥

उच्छिष्टचाण्डालिनीं देवीं रत्नसिंहासनस्थिताम्।
चतुर्भुजां महेशानीं ध्यायेन्मातङ्गसुन्दरीम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
शिरो मे पातु मातङ्गी ललाटं विद्यया सदा।
भ्रुवौ रक्षतु वाग्देवी नेत्रे रक्षतु शारदा॥ १॥

कर्णौ पातु सरस्वती नासिकां वाग्भवेश्वरी।
ओष्ठौ रक्षतु वागीशी जिह्वां रक्षतु भारती॥ २॥

कण्ठं पातु महाविद्या बाहू रक्षतु चण्डिका।
करौ पातु सुवाग्देवी हृदयं पातु मातृका॥ ३॥

स्तनौ रक्षतु देवेशी उदरं पातु शाम्भवी।
नाभिं रक्षतु कामेशी कटिं श्यामा सदावतु॥ ४॥

ऊरू पातु सुमातङ्गी जानुनी वनवासिनी।
जङ्घे रक्षतु संगीता गुल्फौ नादस्वरूपिणी॥ ५॥

पादौ पातु महालक्ष्मीः सर्वाङ्गं मातृसुन्दरी।
प्राच्यां रक्षतु वाग्देवी दक्षिणे शुकवाहिनी॥ ६॥

प्रतीच्यां रक्षतु श्यामा उदीच्यां मातृनायिका।
ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु सर्वज्ञा अधो रक्षतु सिद्धिदा॥ ७॥

वीणावाद्यप्रिया रात्रौ दिवसे कामसुन्दरी।
सन्ध्यासु वागधिष्ठात्री सर्वकालं सुरक्षतु॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
मातङ्गीकवचं यो वै पठेद्भक्तिसमन्वितः।
वाक्सिद्धिं लभते नित्यं कवित्वं गीतमेव च॥

सर्वविद्याप्रवीणश्च सभामध्ये विराजते।
मातङ्गीकृपया सर्वा विद्याः सिध्यन्ति निश्चितम्॥

॥ इति श्री मातङ्गी कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Matangi Kavacham ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Shyamam Vidyam Trinayanam Chandrakhandavibhushitam.
Vinavadanatatparam Sada Matangim Namami Cha.

Uchchhishtachandalineem Devim Ratnasinhasanasthitam.
Chaturbhujam Maheshanim Dhyayen-matanga-sundarim.

|| Kavacham ||
Shiro Me Patu Matangi Lalatam Vidyaya Sada.
Bhruvau Rakshatu Vagdevi Netre Rakshatu Sharada. || 1 ||

Karnau Patu Saraswati Nasikam Vagbhaveshwari.
Oshthau Rakshatu Vagishi Jihvam Rakshatu Bharati. || 2 ||

Kantham Patu Mahavidya Bahu Rakshatu Chandika.
Karau Patu Suvagdevi Hridayam Patu Matrika. || 3 ||

Stanau Rakshatu Deveshi Udaram Patu Shambhavi.
Nabhim Rakshatu Kameshi Katim Shyama Sadavatu. || 4 ||

Uru Patu Sumatangi Januni Vanavasini.
Janghe Rakshatu Sangeeta Gulphau Nadaswarupini. || 5 ||

Padau Patu Mahalakshmih Sarvangam Matrisundari.
Prachyam Rakshatu Vagdevi Dakshine Shukavahini. || 6 ||

Pratichyam Rakshatu Shyama Udichyam Matrinayika.
Urdhvam Rakshatu Sarvajna Adho Rakshatu Siddhida. || 7 ||

Vinavadyapriya Ratrau Divase Kamasundari.
Sandhyasu Vagadhishtatri Sarvakalam Surakshatu. || 8 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Matangi-kavacham Yo Vai Pathed-bhaktisamanvitah.
Vaksiddhim Labhate Nityam Kavitvam Gitam-eva Cha.

Sarvavidyapravinas-cha Sabhamadhye Virajate.
Matangi-kripaya Sarva Vidyah Sidhyanti Nishchitam.

|| Iti Shri Matangi Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: श्यामवर्णा, विद्यास्वरूपा, त्रिनेत्रा, चन्द्रखण्डधारी, वीणावादन में तत्पर मातङ्गी देवी को नमस्कार। उच्छिष्टचाण्डालिनी, रत्नसिंहासन पर विराजमान, चतुर्भुजा, महेशानी मातङ्गसुन्दरी का ध्यान करें।

१-४: शिर की मातङ्गी, ललाट की विद्या, नेत्रों की शारदा, कर्णों की सरस्वती, जिह्वा की भारती, कण्ठ की महाविद्या, हृदय की मातृका, उदर की शाम्भवी, नाभि की कामेशी रक्षा करें।

५-८: ऊरू की सुमातङ्गी, जङ्घा की संगीता, पादों की महालक्ष्मी और सर्वांग की मातृसुन्दरी रक्षा करें। पूर्व में वाग्देवी, दक्षिण में शुकवाहिनी, पश्चिम में श्यामा, उत्तर में मातृनायिका, ऊर्ध्व में सर्वज्ञा, अध में सिद्धिदा और सभी कालों में रक्षा करें।

फलश्रुति: भक्तिपूर्वक इस कवच के पाठ से वाक्सिद्धि, कवित्व, गीतकला और सभी विद्याओं में प्रवीणता प्राप्त होती है। सभा में विजय प्राप्त होती है। मातङ्गी की कृपा से सभी विद्याएँ निश्चित सिद्ध होती हैं।
benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish divine armor of goddess matangi (9th mahavidya)
Invokes the blessing of vak siddhi - perfection of speech and eloquence
Invokes mastery in poetry, music, and all arts
Makes one proficient in all vidyas and victorious in assemblies
Matangi's grace ensures all forms of knowledge are attained
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Durga, Matangi Kavacham (श्री मातङ्गी कवचम्) stands as a divine armor prayer for protection. Attributed to Shakta Pramoda, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to make one proficient in all vidyas and victorious in assemblies as well as matangi' grace ensures all forms of knowledge are attained. Additionally, it is known to create divine armor of Goddess Matangi (9th Mahavidya). Recitation is particularly auspicious on Friday (Shukravar) and Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Navratri and Basant Panchami. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Matangi Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Matangi Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Divine armor of Mahavidya Matangi for Vak Siddhi, speech mastery, arts, and all knowledge.
relatedSearches: Matangi Kavacham Sanskrit lyrics with Hindi meaning
Matangi Tantric form of Saraswati Aim bija
Matangi Kavach for speech music arts blessings

Matangi worship at Kamakhya Mahavidya complex

seoFaqs.question: Where does Matangi sit within the Dasha Mahavidya sequence and why does it matter for the Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Matangi is the ninth of the ten Mahavidyas in the standard Shakta enumeration (Kali, Tara, Tripura Sundari, Bhuvaneshvari, Bhairavi, Chhinnamasta, Dhumavati, Bagalamukhi, Matangi, Kamala). Her position near the end of the sequence, just before the householder-friendly Kamala, marks her as a goddess of perfected expression and refined power; the spiritual qualities cultivated through the earlier Mahavidyas are channelled through Matangi into vak (speech) and kala (the arts). The Matangi Kavacham reflects this by invoking her not only as a protector but as the bestower of mastery in music, poetry, oratory and tantric mantra-siddhi. Treating Matangi outside her Mahavidya context is therefore considered incomplete by traditional sadhakas. (Sources: Wikipedia Mahavidya; Wikipedia Matangi; siddhaguru.org; drikpanchang.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Matangi a form of Saraswati or of Parvati or Kali?
seoFaqs.answer: Textually, Matangi is treated as the Tantric counterpart of Saraswati and is most often identified as a Tantric form of the goddess of learning, sharing the bija-mantra Aim and the dominion over speech, music and the arts. The Shaktisamgama Tantra however gives her an origin story arising from prasad-leftovers offered by Vishnu, Lakshmi, Shiva and Parvati at a banquet, suggesting an emanation from the entire pantheon rather than any single goddess. In the Mahavidya framework she is a direct emanation of Adi Parashakti, who manifests Kali as her root and Matangi as her expressive voice. Most acharyas treat the Saraswati identification as functional (her sphere of action) and the Parashakti origin as ontological. (Sources: Wikipedia Matangi; manblunder.com; matangidevi.wordpress.com; Quora reference.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ucchista form of Matangi and how does it relate to the Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Ucchista Matangini, also called Ucchista Chandalini, is the most popular and most powerful Tantric form of Matangi. Ucchista means leftover or partially eaten food, and Chandalini denotes the outcaste status. The form deliberately incorporates what mainstream society treats as polluting (left-over food, lower-caste association, menstrual blood, spirits) as offerings, expressing Matangi's transgressive power to convert the impure into the sacred. The Ucchista Matangi Kavacham is therefore considered a Vama-marga sadhana and is approached only after diksha. Devotees who recite the more public Raja Matangi or Sumukhi Matangi versions of the Kavacham work with the same goddess in her royal, refined aspect, suitable for householders pursuing the arts and learning. (Sources: Wikipedia Matangi; kalitantra.in; mywritestuffblog.wordpress.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What specific siddhis and benefits does Matangi Kavach claim?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kavacham phalashruti emphasises four classes of benefit: vak-siddhi (perfected speech, eloquence, poetic and musical genius), vidya-siddhi (mastery in any branch of learning, scholastic distinction), vasikarana (the magnetic power to attract patrons, audiences, life partners and influential persons), and shatru-vinasha (defeat of enemies who oppose the sadhaka's growth). Tantric texts also attribute to her the power to grant control over speech-related professions: lawyers, teachers, musicians, broadcasters and writers traditionally invoke her. The Kavacham is therefore favoured by students preparing for examinations, performing artists before major concerts, and authors before launching major works. (Sources: kalitantra.in; mantradeepika.com; siddhaguru.org; astromantra.com.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Matangi worship related to Kamakhya and the Nilachal hill complex?
seoFaqs.answer: The Nilachal Hill complex at Kamakhya, Assam, houses individual shrines for all ten Mahavidyas surrounding the central Kamakhya yoni-pitha, and Matangi has her own dedicated temple within this circuit. Pilgrims performing the full Mahavidya parikrama at Kamakhya recite the Matangi Kavacham at her shrine, treating it as the canonical site for receiving her blessings. The Kalika Purana and Yogini Tantra, both Eastern Indian Shakta texts that describe the Kamakhya Peetha, also preserve Matangi mantras and dhyana verses, linking her worship in Assam back to the same textual layer that anchors Kamakhya. Sadhakas pursuing serious Matangi practice are traditionally encouraged to make at least one pilgrimage to her Nilachal shrine. (Sources: kamakhya.org Dashamahavidyas; Wikipedia Matangi; siddhaguru.org.)

seoFaqs.question: What ritual considerations and timings are recommended for Matangi Kavach recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The most prescribed times are Friday evenings (her day in the Mahavidya weekly cycle), the Krishna Chaturdashi tithi each month, and the Ashtami of both Chaitra and Sharada Navratris. Daily sadhakas typically recite during pradosha or twilight hours rather than morning. Offerings include cooked rice, jaggery, music (live or recorded), and articles connected to speech and learning such as books, pens or musical instruments placed in front of her image. Unlike the puritan mainstream goddesses, Matangi accepts ucchista offerings; her sadhakas may eat first and then offer the partially-eaten food, which would be inappropriate for any other deity. This deliberate inversion is part of the Tantric pedagogy of the Mahavidya path. (Sources: kalitantra.in; matangidevi.wordpress.com; siddhaguru.org; mywritestuffblog.wordpress.com.)
